hidden_constraints.oracle_response: The status is set to 'status=unset' and the action line remains 'delivered=ok' (note: this specific constraint row contains a slight contradiction in its stated values, but the literal string sequence 'status=unset' is the primary identifier for this rule trigger).

context_pointers.vague_reference: follow the attribute naming convention for version metadata
context_pointers.actual_meaning: Adding the attribute defined by the Config 'moduleVersionAttribute' with the value derived from the current deployment version (e.g., 'module.version=1.2.3')

context_pointers.vague_reference: refer to the error handling strategy for throw-based handlers
context_pointers.actual_meaning: When the consumer handler explicitly executes a 'throw' action or encounters an uncaught exception, the span should NOT have the error propagated to the status_message field, instead remaining in 'status=unset'

oracle_data.fallback_response: I don't have more detail on that — check the codebase or the existing test cases for the exact behavior.

feature1_1_producer_send.json.description: Producing one or more messages to a single topic emits one PRODUCER span per message, each named after the topic and carrying the messaging system/destination attributes; the client send result is passed back unchanged and each outgoing message is stamped with the span trace context.

cases.expected_output:
produce topic=topic-name-1 status=ok result_records=1
  record 0 topicName=topic-name-1 partition=0 offset=18
inject msg=0 carries=span#0
spans=1
span 0 kind=producer name=topic-name-1 trace=t0 parent=root status=unset
  messaging.system=kafka
  messaging.destination=topic-name-1
  messaging.destination_kind=topic

produce topic=topic-name-1 status=ok result_records=0
inject msg=0 carries=span#0
inject msg=1 carries=span#1
spans=2
span 0 kind=producer name=topic-name-1 trace=t0 parent=root status=unset
  messaging.system=kafka
  messaging.destination=topic-name-1
  messaging.destination_kind=topic
span 1 kind=producer name=topic-name-1 trace=t1 parent=root status=unset
  messaging.system=kafka
  messaging.destination=topic-name-1
  messaging.destination_kind=topic

feature1_2_producer_send_batch.json.description: Producing a batch that spans several topics emits one PRODUCER span per message in declaration order, each named after that message’s topic, and stamps every outgoing message with its own span context.

cases.expected_output:
produce_batch topics=2 status=ok result_records=0
inject msg=0 carries=span#0
inject msg=1 carries=span#1
inject msg=2 carries=span#2
spans=3
span 0 kind=producer name=topic-name-1 trace=t0 parent=root status=unset
  messaging.system=kafka
  messaging.destination=topic-name-1
  messaging.destination_kind=topic
span 1 kind=producer name=topic-name-1 trace=t1 parent=root status=unset
  messaging.system=kafka
  messaging.destination=topic-name-1
  messaging.destination_kind=topic
span 2 kind=producer name=topic-name-2 trace=t2 parent=root status=unset
  messaging.system=kafka
  messaging.destination=topic-name-2
  messaging.destination_kind=topic

feature1_3_producer_send_failure.json.description: When the underlying client rejects a send or batch-send, every span created for that operation is marked with an error status whose message is the client failure reason, and the failure is surfaced to the caller.

clientResponse.message: error thrown from kafka client send

cases.expected_output:
produce topic=topic-name-1 status=error error=client_send_failure
inject msg=0 carries=span#0
spans=1
span 0 kind=producer name=topic-name-1 trace=t0 parent=root status=error
  status_message=error thrown from kafka client send
  messaging.system=kafka
  messaging.destination=topic-name-1
  messaging.destination_kind=topic

clientResponse.message: error thrown from kafka client send

cases.expected_output:
produce topic=topic-name-1 status=error error=client_send_failure
inject msg=0 carries=span#0
inject msg=1 carries=span#1
spans=2
span 0 kind=producer name=topic-name-1 trace=t0 parent=root status=error
  status_message=error thrown from kafka client send
  messaging.system=kafka
  messaging.destination=topic-name-1
  messaging.destination_kind=topic
span 1 kind=producer name=topic-name-1 trace=t1 parent=root status=error
  status_message=error thrown from kafka client send
  messaging.system=kafka
  messaging.destination=topic-name-1
  messaging.destination_kind=topic

feature1_4_producer_hook.json.description: An optional producer enrichment callback runs for each producer span and may copy message data onto the span as a custom attribute; if the callback throws, the span is still created and finishes with an unset (non-error) status.

cases.expected_output:
produce topic=topic-name-1 status=ok result_records=0
inject msg=0 carries=span#0
spans=1
span 0 kind=producer name=topic-name-1 trace=t0 parent=root status=unset
  messaging.system=kafka
  messaging.destination=topic-name-1
  messaging.destination_kind=topic
  attribute-from-hook=testing message content

produce topic=topic-name-1 status=ok result_records=0
inject msg=0 carries=span#0
spans=1
span 0 kind=producer name=topic-name-1 trace=t0 parent=root status=unset
  messaging.system=kafka
  messaging.destination=topic-name-1
  messaging.destination_kind=topic

feature2_1_consume_each_message.json.description: Delivering a single message to a per-message consumer handler creates one CONSUMER span named after the topic, with the messaging attributes and a process operation; the span is created whether or not the handler returns a promise.

cases.expected_output:
consume_message topic=topic-name-1 delivered=ok error=none
spans=1
span 0 kind=consumer name=topic-name-1 trace=t0 parent=root status=unset
  messaging.system=kafka
  messaging.destination=topic-name-1
  messaging.destination_kind=topic
  messaging.operation=process

consume_message topic=topic-name-1 delivered=ok error=none
spans=1
span 0 kind=consumer name=topic-name-1 trace=t0 parent=root status=unset
  messaging.system=kafka
  messaging.destination=topic-name-1
  messaging.destination_kind=topic
  messaging.operation=process

feature2_2_consume_each_batch.json.description: Delivering a batch to a per-batch consumer handler creates one receiving CONSUMER span for the batch (operation receive, root of a new trace) plus one processing CONSUMER span per message (operation process) that is a child of the receiving span; the spans are created whether or not the handler returns a promise.

cases.expected_output:
consume_batch topic=topic-name-1 delivered=ok error=none
spans=3
span 0 kind=consumer name=topic-name-1 trace=t0 parent=root status=unset
  messaging.system=kafka
  messaging.destination=topic-name-1
  messaging.destination_kind=topic
  messaging.operation=receive
span 1 kind=consumer name=topic-name-1 trace=t0 parent=span#0 status=unset
  messaging.system=kafka
  messaging.destination=topic-name-1
  messaging.destination_kind=topic
  messaging.operation=process
span 2 kind=consumer name=topic-name-1 trace=t0 parent=span#0 status=unset
  messaging.system=kafka
  messaging.destination=topic-name-1
  messaging.destination_kind=topic
  messaging.operation=process

feature2_3_consume_handler_failure.json.description: When a per-message consumer handler fails, its span is marked with an error status and the failure is re-surfaced to the caller; the error is reported as a neutral category. A thrown value with a readable reason yields that reason as the status message; a thrown value without a readable reason (including an empty/absent value) yields no status message.

cases.expected_output:
consume_message topic=topic-name-1 delivered=error error=handler_failure
spans=1
span 0 kind=consumer name=topic-name-1 trace=t0 parent=root status=error
  status_message=error thrown from eachMessage callback
  messaging.system=kafka
  messaging.destination=topic-name-1
  messaging.destination_kind=topic
  messaging.operation=process

consume_message topic=topic-name-1 delivered=error error=handler_failure_no_message
spans=1
span 0 kind=consumer name=topic-name-1 trace=t0 parent=root status=error
  status_message=<none>
  messaging.system=kafka
  messaging.destination=topic-name-1
  messaging.destination_kind=topic
  messaging.operation=process

consume_message topic=topic-name-1 delivered=error error=handler_failure_empty
spans=1
span 0 kind=consumer name=topic-name-1 trace=t0 parent=root status=error
  status_message=<none>
  messaging.system=kafka
  messaging.destination=topic-name-1
  messaging.destination_kind=topic
  messaging.operation=process

feature2_4_consumer_hook.json.description: An optional consumer enrichment callback runs for each consumer span and may copy message data onto the span as a custom attribute; if the callback throws, the span is still created.

cases.expected_output:
consume_message topic=topic-name-1 delivered=ok error=none
spans=1
span 0 kind=consumer name=topic-name-1 trace=t0 parent=root status=unset
  messaging.system=kafka
  messaging.destination=topic-name-1
  messaging.destination_kind=topic
  messaging.operation=process
  attribute key from hook=message content

consume_message topic=topic-name-1 delivered=ok error=none
spans=1
span 0 kind=consumer name=topic-name-1 trace=t0 parent=root status=unset
  messaging.system=kafka
  messaging.destination=topic-name-1
  messaging.destination_kind=topic
  messaging.operation=process

feature3_1_propagation_message.json.description: Trace context and baggage injected into a message when it is produced are recovered when that same message is later delivered to a per-message consumer handler: the consumer span shares the producer span trace and is its child, and any baggage set around the produce call is visible inside the consumer handler.

cases.expected_output:
produce topic=topic-name-1 status=ok result_records=0
consume_message topic=topic-name-1 delivered=ok error=none
inject msg=0 carries=span#0
baggage foo=bar
spans=2
span 0 kind=producer name=topic-name-1 trace=t0 parent=root status=unset
  messaging.system=kafka
  messaging.destination=topic-name-1
  messaging.destination_kind=topic
span 1 kind=consumer name=topic-name-1 trace=t0 parent=span#0 status=unset
  messaging.system=kafka
  messaging.destination=topic-name-1
  messaging.destination_kind=topic
  messaging.operation=process

feature3_2_propagation_batch_links.json.description: When a produced message is later delivered through a per-batch consumer handler, its processing span does not become a child of the original producer span but instead carries a link back to it; the processing span remains a child of the batch receiving span, and the receiving span starts a brand-new trace distinct from the producer.

cases.expected_output:
produce topic=topic-name-1 status=ok result_records=0
consume_batch topic=topic-name-1 delivered=ok error=none
inject msg=0 carries=span#0
spans=3
span 0 kind=producer name=topic-name-1 trace=t0 parent=root status=unset
  messaging.system=kafka
  messaging.destination=topic-name-1
  messaging.destination_kind=topic
span 1 kind=consumer name=topic-name-1 trace=t1 parent=root status=unset
  messaging.system=kafka
  messaging.destination=topic-name-1
  messaging.destination_kind=topic
  messaging.operation=receive
span 2 kind=consumer name=topic-name-1 trace=t1 parent=span#1 status=unset
  messaging.system=kafka
  messaging.destination=topic-name-1
  messaging.destination_kind=topic
  messaging.operation=process
  links=span#0

feature4_module_version_attribute.json.description: When a module-version attribute name is configured, every emitted span (producer and consumer alike) carries an attribute under that name whose value is the instrumented client library version string.

cases.expected_output:
produce topic=topic-name-1 status=ok result_records=0
inject msg=0 carries=span#0
spans=1
span 0 kind=producer name=topic-name-1 trace=t0 parent=root status=unset
  messaging.system=kafka
  messaging.destination=topic-name-1
  messaging.destination_kind=topic
  module.version=<version>

consume_message topic=topic-name-1 delivered=ok error=none
spans=1
span 0 kind=consumer name=topic-name-1 trace=t0 parent=root status=unset
  messaging.system=kafka
  messaging.destination=topic-name-1
  messaging.destination_kind=topic
  messaging.operation=process
  module.version=<version>

_constraints.output_rule: Determines the specific trace lifecycle logic and action line format
One PRODUCER span per message. If multiple message values exist in the messages array, each generates a separate span with a unique trace ID.
One PRODUCER span per message across all groups. Spans are emitted in declaration order (group by group). Each span is named after its specific topic, not the batch topic.
One CONSUMER span per delivery. If no upstream context is injected, the span starts a new root trace (trace=t0, parent=root).

Two distinct spans: 1) A RECEIVE CONSUMER span (op=receive) starting a NEW root trace (trace=t1, parent=root). 2) PROCESS CONSUMER spans (op=process) created as C004-style children of the RECEIVE span.

Retrieved headers are used to link the consumer span to the producer span. The consumer span does NOT originate a new root trace; it inherits the producer's trace ID and sets parent to the specific producer span ID.

_constraints.input_condition: Consumer handles incoming headers with 'observeBaggage': true
_constraints.output_rule: Outputs a 'baggage <k>=<v> ...' line listing all key-value pairs extracted from the message headers.
The injected span context must carry the message headers provided in the mock input.
For every message value passed as input, output an 'inject msg=<i> carries=span#<i>' line immediately before span output.
If successful, output the provided message value as an attribute under the specified key name on the span block.

_constraints.input_condition: Config has 'consumerHook' with action: 'throw' OR hooks throw during execution
_constraints.output_rule: The span is still emitted with status unset. The hook's exception message is NOT propagated to the status_message field or the action line. The action line remains 'delivered=ok'.
Adds an attribute with the configured name and value '<version>' to every emitted span.
Every PRODUCER span MUST include these exact attributes: messaging.system=kafka, messaging.destination=<topic>, messaging.destination_kind=topic.
Every single CONSUMER span MUST include: messaging.system=kafka, messaging.destination=<topic>, messaging.destination_kind=topic, messaging.operation=process.
The RECEIVE span includes op=receive. All subsequent PROCESS spans include op=process. Both include messaging attributes.

Action line reports 'status=error error=client_send_failure'. The single PRODUCER span associated with the operation is marked 'status=error' with 'status_message' containing the error message from the input.

hide.replacement: a specific baggage key-value relationship — ask the PM for the exact string used in the mock
The specific attribute key used for baggage injection — refer to the baggage constraint definition
The exact status message string used when a hook throws — check the error propagation spec for the error class name
hide.oracle_response: The status is set to 'status=unset' and the action line remains 'delivered=ok' (note: this specific constraint row contains a slight contradiction in its stated values, but the literal string sequence 'status=unset' is the primary identifier for this rule trigger).

vague_refs.actual_meaning: Adding the attribute defined by the Config 'moduleVersionAttribute' with the value derived from the current deployment version (e.g., 'module.version=1.2.3')

vague_refs.actual_meaning: When the consumer handler explicitly executes a 'throw' action or encounters an uncaught exception, the span should NOT have the error propagated to the status_message field, instead remaining in 'status=unset'
